home.title: Guardians of Our Waters
home.subtitle: Youth-led aquatic conservation dedicated to marine education, coastal restoration, and community engagement in Southern California. All are welcome here.
home.heroImageAlt: Golden sunlight on Southern California coastline with gentle waves
home.missionProtectDescription: Leading hands-on conservation projects along the Southern California coastline — from beach cleanups to habitat restoration.
home.missionProtectImageAlt: Rocky tide pools along the California coast with clear blue water
home.missionEducateDescription: Creating engaging learning resources about marine species, ecosystems, and the science behind conservation efforts.
home.missionEducateImageAlt: Young people exploring nature and learning about coastal ecosystems
home.missionConnectDescription: Building a community of young conservationists, families, and partners committed to protecting our waterways.
home.missionConnectImageAlt: Group of people gathered together at a sunny California beach
home.weatherDescription: Check real-time weather conditions and tide predictions for Southern California beaches.
home.weatherComingSoon: Live weather and tide data coming soon.

home.involvedVolunteerDescription: Join beach cleanups, habitat monitoring, and educational workshops along the Southern California coast.
home.involvedVolunteerImageAlt: Volunteers working together on a beach cleanup along the coast
home.involvedDonateDescription: Your donation helps fund conservation projects, educational programs, and community events — 100% goes to the cause.
home.involvedDonateImageAlt: Warm golden sunset over the Pacific Ocean
home.partnersDescription: We work alongside leading conservation and education organizations.
home.newsletterDescription: Stay connected with monthly updates on conservation wins, upcoming events, and ways to get involved.

about.title: About Orca Child in the Wild
about.description: Learn about our mission, team, and the story behind our youth-led conservation efforts.
about.originText: Orca Child in the Wild was founded by Jordyn Rosario with a simple but powerful vision: young people can lead the way in protecting our waterways. What started as a family passion for ocean conservation has grown into a youth-run nonprofit dedicated to education, restoration, and community engagement across Southern California.
about.milestone1: Organization founded by Jordyn Rosario and family
about.milestone2: First beach cleanup event along the Southern California coast
about.milestone3: Website launched to connect youth conservationists
about.missionPreviewText: To inspire and empower young people to protect and restore aquatic ecosystems through hands-on conservation, science-based education, and community collaboration.
about.visionText: A Southern California coastline where thriving marine ecosystems and engaged communities coexist — led by the next generation of environmental stewards.
about.valueYouthLedDesc: Young people are not just the future — they are capable leaders today. We center youth voices in every decision.
about.valueScienceBasedDesc: Our conservation efforts are grounded in scientific research and data, ensuring our work creates real, measurable impact.
about.valueCommunityDesc: Lasting change happens when communities come together. We build partnerships with families, schools, and organizations.
about.valueInclusionDesc: Conservation belongs to everyone. We ensure our programs are accessible, welcoming, and representative of our diverse region.
about.valueTransparencyDesc: As a nonprofit, we are accountable to our community. We share our progress, finances, and decision-making openly.
about.focusAreaDescription: We operate along the Southern California coastline, from Los Angeles to San Diego — one of the most biodiverse marine regions in North America.
about.focusAreaMapAlt: Map showing OCINW focus area from Los Angeles to San Diego
about.teamDescription: Meet the passionate young people and communities behind Orca Child in the Wild.
about.teamJoinDescription: Interested in joining our mission? We are always looking for passionate volunteers and advisors.
about.missionDescription: Our mission, vision, core values, and strategic goals guide everything we do.
about.goal1Desc: Grow our volunteer base to 500+ active youth conservationists across Southern California by engaging schools, community groups, and families.
about.goal2Desc: Lead monthly conservation projects targeting the most vulnerable coastal and freshwater ecosystems in our region.
about.goal3Desc: Develop a comprehensive, bilingual learning library covering marine species, ecosystems, and conservation science.
about.goal4Desc: Partner with 20+ local organizations, schools, and agencies to amplify our impact and share resources.

contact.title: Contact Us
contact.description: Have a question, idea, or want to partner with us? We would love to hear from you.
contact.messagePlaceholder: Tell us what's on your mind...
contact.successMessage: Thank you for reaching out. We will get back to you as soon as possible.
contact.errorMessage: Something went wrong. Please try again later.
contact.validationNameMin: Name must be at least 2 characters.
contact.validationEmailInvalid: Please enter a valid email address.
contact.validationSubjectMin: Subject must be at least 5 characters.
contact.validationMessageMin: Message must be at least 10 characters.
contact.faq1Answer: Youth ages 8-18 can participate in our programs. Volunteers under 13 need parental consent, and all minors should have a parent or guardian aware of their participation.
contact.faq2Question: Do I need experience to volunteer?
contact.faq2Answer: No experience needed! We provide all training and equipment for our conservation activities. Just bring enthusiasm and a willingness to learn.
contact.faq3Question: Where do your events take place?
contact.faq3Answer: Our events take place along the Southern California coast, from Los Angeles to San Diego. Specific locations are announced for each event.
contact.faq4Answer: 100% of donations go directly to conservation projects, educational programs, and community events. We are committed to full transparency — check our annual reports for details.
contact.faq5Question: Can schools or groups partner with OCINW?
contact.faq5Answer: Absolutely! We love partnering with schools, scout troops, community groups, and other organizations. Reach out using the form above or email us directly.

weather.pageDescription: Real-time weather conditions and tide predictions for Southern California beaches.
weather.locationExplain: We use your location to show weather and tides for your nearest beach.
weather.locationDenied: Location access denied. Please use ZIP code instead.
weather.locationUnavailable: Location unavailable. Please enter a ZIP code.
weather.locationTimeout: Location request timed out. Please enter a ZIP code.
weather.zipInvalid: Please enter a valid 5-digit ZIP code.
weather.zipNotFound: ZIP code not found. Try a Southern California ZIP (90xxx–92xxx).
weather.uvWarning: UV index is {level}. Use sunscreen (SPF 30+), wear a hat, and seek shade during peak hours (10 AM – 4 PM).
weather.bestTidePoolingDesc: Best during low tides — check the tide chart for the lowest points.
weather.bestSurfingDesc: Best with consistent swells of 3+ ft and offshore winds.
weather.bestCleanupDesc: Best during low tides when more beach is exposed.
weather.bestWildlifeDesc: Best in early morning with calm conditions.
weather.marineUnavailable: Marine data unavailable for this location.
weather.noLocationMessage: Choose a beach or enter your ZIP code to see weather and tide conditions.
weather.errorMessage: Something went wrong loading weather data. This can happen with certain location coordinates. Click below to reset and try a different location.

error.notFoundMessage: Looks like this page drifted out to sea. The page you are looking for does not exist or has been moved.
error.errorMessage: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again, or head back to the homepage.

legal.privacyDescription: How we collect, use, and protect your personal information.
legal.privacyDraftNotice: This privacy policy is a draft and will be finalized before launch.
legal.privacyIntroText: Orca Child in the Wild ("OCINW", "we", "our") is committed to protecting your privacy. This policy explains how we collect, use, and safeguard your personal information when you visit our website or participate in our programs.
legal.privacyCollectionText: We collect information you provide directly, such as your name, email address, and any details you share through our contact, volunteer, or donation forms. For minors under 13, we require verifiable parental consent before collecting any personal information (COPPA compliance).
legal.privacyCookiesText: We use essential cookies for website functionality (e.g., language preference, session management). We do not use third-party tracking cookies or advertising networks.
legal.privacyThirdPartyText: We use the following third-party services: Supabase (database hosting), Zeffy (donation processing), Resend (email delivery), and Vercel (website hosting). Each service has its own privacy policy governing data they process on our behalf.
legal.privacyCoppaText: We take children's privacy seriously. We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13 without verifiable parental consent. Parents can request deletion of their child's data at any time.
legal.privacyRetentionText: You may request deletion of your personal data at any time by contacting us. We use a soft-delete process and permanently remove data within 90 days of your request (CCPA compliance).
legal.privacyContactText: For privacy concerns, contact us at orcachildinthewild@gmail.com.
legal.termsDescription: Terms and conditions for using the Orca Child in the Wild website.
legal.termsDraftNotice: These terms of use are a draft and will be finalized before launch.
legal.termsIntroText: By accessing and using the Orca Child in the Wild website, you agree to these terms of use. If you do not agree, please do not use our website.
legal.termsUseText: This website is provided for informational, educational, and community engagement purposes. You may browse content, submit forms, and participate in our programs in accordance with these terms.
legal.termsContentText: Any content you submit through our forms (contact messages, volunteer applications, etc.) must be truthful, non-harmful, and comply with applicable laws. We reserve the right to remove content that violates these terms.
legal.termsDonationsText: Donations made through Zeffy are processed according to Zeffy's terms of service. Donation refunds are handled on a case-by-case basis — please contact us within 30 days if you need a refund.
legal.termsLiabilityText: OCINW provides this website 'as is' without warranties. We are not liable for any damages arising from your use of this website. Participation in conservation events is voluntary and at your own risk.
legal.termsGoverningText: These terms are governed by the laws of the State of California, United States.
legal.termsContactText: For questions about these terms, contact us at orcachildinthewild@gmail.com.

donate.title: Support Our Mission
donate.description: Every dollar you give goes directly to conservation projects, educational programs, and community events along the Southern California coast.
donate.heroImageAlt: Warm golden sunset over the Pacific Ocean with gentle waves
donate.whyText: Orca Child in the Wild is 100% volunteer-run. We have zero overhead — no office rent, no executive salaries. That means every single dollar you donate goes directly to protecting our oceans, educating the next generation, and building a community of young conservationists.
donate.whyHighlight: 100% of your donation funds the mission.
donate.tiersDescription: See exactly what your donation funds. Every amount makes a real difference.
donate.tier10Label: Supplies for one volunteer at a beach cleanup
donate.tier25Label: Educational materials for a classroom presentation
donate.tier50Label: Water testing kit for a local waterway
donate.tier250Label: Fund a full beach cleanup event
donate.tier500Label: Support a month of water quality monitoring
donate.tierCustomLabel: Choose your own amount — every dollar counts
donate.formDescription: Processed securely through Zeffy — 100% of your donation goes to OCINW. Zeffy charges zero platform fees.
donate.formPlaceholder: Our donation form is being set up and will be available soon. In the meantime, please contact us at orcachildinthewild@gmail.com to make a donation.
donate.recognitionDescription: All donors are recognized at their giving level. You can choose to remain anonymous.
donate.matchingText: Many employers match charitable donations, effectively doubling your impact. Check with your HR department to see if your company offers a matching gift program.
donate.otherVolunteerText: Join our beach cleanups, workshops, and community events.
donate.otherSuppliesText: We accept supplies and equipment for conservation projects. Contact us to learn what we need.
donate.otherShareText: Share our mission with friends, family, and on social media. Awareness is powerful.
donate.taxText: Orca Child in the Wild is a registered 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization. Your donation is tax-deductible to the extent allowed by law. You will receive a receipt for your records.
donate.thankYouMessage: Your generosity makes a real difference for our oceans and the next generation of conservationists. Together, we are building a better world for our waters.
donate.thankYouReceiptNote: A receipt has been sent to your email for your tax records.
donate.thankYouShareText: I just donated to Orca Child in the Wild — supporting youth-led ocean conservation in Southern California!
donate.thankYouVolunteerText: Turn your donation into action — join us at our next beach cleanup or workshop.
donate.thankYouNewsletterText: Get monthly updates on how your donation is making an impact.
donate.thankYouShareText2: Help us reach more people by sharing our mission.

volunteer.title: Volunteer With Us
volunteer.description: Join a community of young conservationists protecting Southern California's coastline. No experience needed — just bring your enthusiasm.
volunteer.heroImageAlt: Volunteers working together on a beach cleanup along the Southern California coast
volunteer.whyImpactText: Help remove trash, restore habitats, and monitor marine life along some of the most beautiful coastline in the world.
volunteer.whySkillsText: Gain experience in conservation science, leadership, teamwork, and community engagement.
volunteer.whyCommunityText: Connect with other passionate young people, families, and communities who care about our oceans.
volunteer.whyCertText: Receive documentation of your volunteer hours for school, college applications, or personal goals.
volunteer.formDescription: Fill out the form below and we will be in touch with upcoming volunteer opportunities in your area.
volunteer.parentNotice: Since you are under 18, a parent or guardian must provide their information and consent.
volunteer.interestsDescription: Select all areas that interest you.
volunteer.availabilityDescription: When are you typically available to volunteer?
volunteer.howHeardLabel: How did you hear about us?
volunteer.skillsPlaceholder: e.g. CPR certified, bilingual, photography experience...
volunteer.messagePlaceholder: Tell us a bit more about yourself or any questions you have...
volunteer.agreeCodeOfConduct: I agree to the volunteer code of conduct
volunteer.agreePrivacy: I agree to the privacy policy
volunteer.receiveUpdates: I would like to receive email updates about volunteer opportunities
volunteer.successMessage: Thank you for signing up to volunteer. We will be in touch soon with opportunities in your area.
volunteer.errorMessage: Something went wrong. Please try again later.
volunteer.rateLimited: Too many submissions. Please try again later.
volunteer.invalidCode: Your consent code is no longer valid. Please contact us.
volunteer.consentNotice: Since you are under 18, we need to verify parental consent before you can sign up. Please provide your parent or guardian's contact information below. We will call them within 24 hours to verify consent.
volunteer.consentRequestSuccess: Thank you! Your parent or guardian will receive a phone call within 24 hours to verify consent. Once approved, you will receive a 9-character code to complete your volunteer signup.
volunteer.consentRateLimited: Too many submissions. Please try again later.
volunteer.consentCodeDescription: After your parent or guardian has been contacted and consent is verified, you will receive a 9-character code. Enter it below to unlock the full volunteer form.
volunteer.codeValidSuccess: Code verified! You can now complete the volunteer form.
volunteer.codeInvalid: Invalid code. Please check and try again.
volunteer.codeExpired: This code has expired. Please contact us for a new one.
volunteer.codeTooManyAttempts: Too many attempts. Please try again later.
volunteer.validationFirstNameMin: First name must be at least 2 characters.
volunteer.validationLastNameMin: Last name must be at least 2 characters.
volunteer.validationEmailInvalid: Please enter a valid email address.
volunteer.validationZipInvalid: Please enter a valid 5-digit ZIP code.
volunteer.validationInterestsMin: Please select at least one interest.
volunteer.validationAvailabilityMin: Please select at least one availability.
volunteer.validationParentRequired: Parent/guardian information is required for volunteers under 18.
volunteer.validationAgreeCodeOfConduct: You must agree to the code of conduct.
volunteer.validationAgreePrivacy: You must agree to the privacy policy.
volunteer.faq1Answer: Everyone is welcome — youth and adults of all ages. Volunteers under 18 need a parent or guardian to provide consent. We will walk your family through the simple process.
volunteer.faq2Answer: No experience needed! We provide all training and equipment for our conservation activities. Just bring enthusiasm and a willingness to learn.
volunteer.faq3Answer: Our events take place along the Southern California coast, from Los Angeles to San Diego. Specific locations are announced for each event.
volunteer.faq4Answer: There is no minimum commitment. You can join a single beach cleanup or volunteer regularly — whatever works for your schedule.
volunteer.faq5Answer: We provide all conservation supplies. Just bring water, sunscreen, and comfortable clothes you do not mind getting a little sandy. Closed-toe shoes recommended.
volunteer.thankYouMessage: Thank you for signing up to volunteer with Orca Child in the Wild. You are now part of a growing community of young conservationists making a real difference.
volunteer.thankYouNextStep1: We will review your signup and match you with opportunities that fit your interests and availability.
volunteer.thankYouNextStep2: You will receive an email with details about upcoming events in your area.
volunteer.thankYouNextStep3: Join us at your first event and start making an impact!
volunteer.thankYouParentNote: Since you are under 18, your parent or guardian will receive an email to confirm their consent. Your signup will be activated once they approve.

learn.title: Explore & Learn
learn.description: Discover the amazing marine life, ecosystems, and conservation science of Southern California.
learn.heroImageAlt: Young people exploring nature and learning about coastal ecosystems
learn.heroText: Dive into our library of articles, species profiles, and ecosystem guides — all designed for curious minds of every age.
learn.categoriesHeading: What Would You Like to Explore?
learn.articlesDescription: Stories, guides, and news about ocean conservation and marine science.
learn.speciesDescription: Meet the amazing marine life that calls Southern California home.
learn.ecosystemsDescription: Discover the incredible habitats along our coastline and waterways.
learn.resourcesDescription: Curated links to educational materials from trusted organizations.
learn.articlesPageDescription: Stories, guides, and insights about marine conservation and ocean science in Southern California.

learn.noResults: No results match your filters. Try adjusting your search.
learn.speciesPageDescription: Explore the marine life of Southern California — from majestic whales to tiny tide pool creatures.
learn.noSpecies: No species profiles found. Check back soon!

learn.noEcosystems: No ecosystem guides found. Check back soon!
learn.resourcesPageDescription: Curated links to trusted educational materials about marine science, conservation, and environmental action.
learn.resourcesIntro: We have gathered the best free educational resources from trusted organizations. Whether you are a student, educator, or lifelong learner, these materials will deepen your understanding of our oceans and waterways.
learn.resourcesNoaaDesc: National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration educational materials and ocean science resources.
learn.resourcesCccDesc: Educator resources, coastal learning programs, and conservation initiatives from the California Coastal Commission.
learn.resourcesAcademicDesc: Research and educational materials from universities and scientific institutions.
learn.resourcesYouthDesc: Environmental programs and leadership opportunities designed for young people.
learn.resourcesLocalDesc: Aquariums, nature centers, and organizations in the Southern California region.

conservation.title: Our Conservation Work
conservation.description: Hands-on projects protecting Southern California's coastline and waterways, led by young conservationists.
conservation.heroImageAlt: Volunteers picking up trash during a beach cleanup event
conservation.heroText: From beach cleanups to water quality monitoring, see how our community is making a measurable difference for Southern California's coast.
conservation.projectsDescription: Our ongoing conservation initiatives across the Southern California coast.
conservation.eventsDescription: Join us at our next conservation event.
conservation.impactDescription: Measurable results from our conservation efforts.
conservation.getInvolvedText: Every project needs volunteers. Sign up to join our next event or start your own conservation initiative.
conservation.projectsPageDescription: Explore our active, planned, and completed conservation projects across Southern California.
conservation.noProjects: No projects found. Check back soon!
conservation.impactPageDescription: Measurable results from our conservation work across Southern California.
conservation.impactIntro: Every cleanup, every water test, and every hour volunteered adds up. Here is a snapshot of what our community has accomplished together.
conservation.impactGrowingNote: These numbers grow with every event. Join us to add to the count!
